questions.question: Who is the author of the story 'A Tiger in the House'?
questions.explanation: The story is written by the famous author Ruskin Bond.

questions.question: Where did Grandfather discover the tiger cub?

options.B: In the Terai Jungle near Dehra
options.D: Near a banyan tree in his garden

questions.explanation: Timothy was discovered on a hunting expedition in the Terai Jungle near Dehra.

questions.question: What was the length of the tiger cub when it was discovered?
questions.explanation: Grandfather discovered a little tiger about eighteen inches long hiding among roots.

questions.question: Who named the tiger cub 'Timothy'?
questions.explanation: The tiger cub was named Timothy by Grandmother.

questions.question: Initially, what was Timothy's primary diet?
questions.explanation: At first, he was brought up entirely on milk given to him by the cook.

questions.question: Who was Timothy's cook and caretaker at home?
questions.explanation: Mahmoud was the cook who gave Timothy milk in a feeding bottle.

questions.question: Which of these animals was NOT a companion of Timothy at home?
questions.explanation: The leopard was in the cage next to the tiger at the zoo, not a companion at home.

questions.question: How did Toto the monkey tease Timothy?

options.B: By pulling him by the tail

questions.explanation: Toto was bold enough to pull the young tiger by the tail.

questions.question: Where would Toto climb if Timothy lost his temper?
questions.explanation: Toto would climb up the curtains if Timothy lost his temper.

questions.question: Timothy's favorite place in the house was the ________.
questions.explanation: His favourite place in the house was the drawing room, specifically the long sofa.

questions.question: When Timothy was six months old, what major change occurred?
questions.explanation: When he was about six months old, a change came over him; he grew steadily less friendly.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather decide to transfer Timothy to a zoo?

options.A: Because the writer left for Dehra
options.C: Because he began to stalk Mahmoud with villainous intent
options.D: Because Grandmother was afraid of him

questions.explanation: Grandfather decided it was time to transfer him when he began to stalk Mahmoud with what looked like villainous intent.

questions.question: In which city was the zoo where Timothy was sent?
questions.explanation: The nearest zoo was at Lucknow, two hundred miles away.

questions.question: How did Grandfather transport Timothy to the zoo?

options.D: In a cage on an elephant

questions.explanation: Grandfather reserved a first-class compartment for himself and Timothy.

questions.question: How long after sending Timothy to the zoo did Grandfather visit him?
questions.explanation: Grandfather took the opportunity to visit the zoo about six months later.

questions.question: What did Grandfather do when the tiger growled during the visit?

options.B: He smacked him across the mouth

questions.explanation: Whenever he growled, Grandfather smacked him across the mouth, which was his old way of keeping him quiet.

questions.question: Which animal in the next cage kept frightening the tiger at the zoo?
questions.explanation: The tiger sprang away when a leopard in the next cage snarled at him.

questions.question: What shocking information did the keeper give Grandfather?

options.B: Timothy was transferred to another zoo
options.C: Timothy had died two months ago

questions.explanation: The keeper informed Grandfather that Timothy had died two months ago of pneumonia.

questions.question: According to the keeper, what caused Timothy's death?
questions.explanation: The keeper said, 'He died two months ago of pneumonia.'

questions.question: Where did the dangerous tiger in the cage come from?

options.A: It was raised in the zoo
options.B: Trapped in the hills only last month

questions.explanation: The keeper explained that the current tiger was trapped in the hills only last month.

questions.question: The tiger at the zoo was licking Grandfather’s arm with increasing _______.
questions.explanation: The text says, 'The tiger was still licking his arm, with increasing relish.'

questions.question: How did Grandfather feel when he left the zoo?
questions.explanation: He gave the keeper a scornful look and walked briskly out of the zoo.

questions.question: Grandfather was requested to accompany the hunting party because ______.

options.B: He knew the forests of Siwalik hills better than most
options.C: He wanted to catch a tiger
options.D: He was the oldest person in Delhi

questions.explanation: He knew the land and the direction beaters should take better than others.

questions.question: What was the weight or size of Timothy when the writer lived with Grandfather?
questions.explanation: He was by this time the size of a full-grown retriever.

questions.question: Timothy slept at night in the _______.
questions.explanation: He slept at night in the cook's quarters.

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'shikari' in the context of the text?
questions.explanation: The text mentions Grandfather was no shikari (hunter).

questions.question: Which animal was 'bold enough to pull the young tiger by the tail'?
questions.explanation: Toto the monkey was bold enough to pull Timothy by the tail.

questions.question: The hunting party consisted of several important persons from _______.
questions.explanation: The party consisted of several very important persons from Delhi.

questions.question: How many elephants were in the hunting camp?
questions.explanation: There were also some fifteen elephants in the camp.

questions.question: What was the 'villainous intent' Timothy showed towards Mahmoud?

options.A: He wanted to play with him
options.B: He began to stalk him about the house

questions.explanation: He began to stalk Mahmoud with what looked like villainous intent.

questions.question: In the zoo, Grandfather found the tiger in a cage _______ in a corner.
questions.explanation: The tiger was there, crouched in a corner, full-grown.

questions.question: What did the tiger do to Grandfather's hands at the zoo?
questions.explanation: He licked Grandfather's hands and only sprang away when the leopard snarled.

questions.question: Which of these is a textbook glossary meaning for 'expedition'?
questions.explanation: Glossary defines expedition as a party / trip.

questions.question: What does 'intricate' mean in the description of the banyan tree?

options.B: Having a lot of different parts and small details

questions.explanation: Glossary defines intricate as having many parts/details that fit together.

questions.question: A 'mongrel' is a kind of _______.
questions.explanation: Glossary: mongrel - a kind of dog (mixture of different breeds).

questions.question: The word 'stalk' means to follow _______.
questions.explanation: Glossary: stalk - follow stealthily / quietly.

questions.question: What is a 'howdah' used for?

options.B: Riding on the back of an elephant

questions.explanation: Glossary: howdah - a seat for riding on the back of an elephant.

questions.question: Grandfather was strolling down the forest path when he discovered the _______ tiger.
questions.explanation: The text mentions he 'discovered a little tiger about eighteen inches long'.

questions.question: Why did people on the road give Timothy and the writer a 'wide berth'?

options.B: Because they were afraid of the tiger

questions.explanation: When the writer took him for walks, people gave a wide berth out of fear of the wild animal.

questions.question: How did the writer become one of the tiger's favorites?

options.C: By letting him sleep on the bed

questions.explanation: Timothy's favorite amusement was to stalk anyone who played with him, and the writer played with him.

questions.question: The second keeper observed Grandfather with some _______.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'another keeper observing him with some alarm.'

questions.question: What happened to the poultry house at night as Timothy grew older?

options.B: Frenzied cackling was heard and feathers were found
options.D: The writer locked him in there

questions.explanation: Frenzied cackling would be heard and in the morning feathers would be lying on the veranda.

questions.question: Grandfather recognized the second keeper as the one who was there when _______.

options.B: Timothy first came to the zoo

questions.explanation: Grandfather recognized him as the keeper who had been there when Timothy first came.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather say 'Goodnight, Timothy' at the end?

options.B: To say a final goodbye, still identifying with his pet
options.C: Because it was time for the tiger to sleep
options.D: Because the tiger told him to

questions.explanation: It was a poignant final farewell to his memory of Timothy, even though he knew this wasn't him.

questions.question: According to the story, who licked the tiger's forehead and ticked his ear?
questions.explanation: At the zoo, Grandfather stroked the tiger's forehead and ticked his ear.

questions.question: What is the size of Timothy at the end of the story in the zoo?
questions.explanation: The tiger was 'full-grown and with a magnificent striped coat.'

questions.question: The nearest zoo at Lucknow was _______ miles away.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions 'two hundred miles away.'

questions.question: Grandfather went in search of the _______ of the zoo.
questions.explanation: He went to suggest putting the tiger elsewhere to the Superintendent.

questions.question: The tiger Grandfather petted at the zoo was trapped _______.
questions.explanation: The keeper said: 'This tiger was trapped in the hills only last month.'

questions.question: Why did Grandfather withdraw his hand from the cage with 'increasing relish' from the tiger?

options.B: The tiger was dangerous and licking his arm

questions.explanation: The tiger was dangerous and the realization made the withdrawal take 'what seemed to him an age.'

questions.question: Timothy's clean habits included scrubbing his face with his paws exactly like a _______.
questions.explanation: The text compares his face-scrubbing habit to that of a cat.

questions.question: Grandfather was the only member of the party to have _______ any game.
questions.explanation: He had the distinction of being the only member to have bagged any game.

questions.question: What did the sportsmen begin to shoot at when they didn't find a tiger?
questions.explanation: They were beginning to shoot at jackals when Grandfather discovered Timothy.

questions.question: Timothy would creep closer to the writer and suddenly make a dash for his _______.
questions.explanation: He would 'suddenly making a dash for my feet.'

questions.question: Who declared 'one of these days we are going to find Timothy sitting on Mahmoud’s bed'?
questions.explanation: Grandfather declared this in his prophetic manner.

questions.question: Grandfather was 'no _______', but he knew the forests well.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Grandfather was no shikari.'

questions.question: The zoo authorities were _______ to receive a well-fed tiger.
questions.explanation: The text says they were 'only too glad to receive as a gift a well fed and fairly civilized tiger.'

questions.question: Grandfather walked out of the zoo _______ after the encounter.
questions.explanation: The last line says he 'walked briskly out of the zoo.'

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'impressive' in the glossary?
questions.explanation: Glossary: sumptuous : impressive.

questions.question: What does 'crouch' mean according to the glossary?

options.B: To bend the knees and bring the upper body forward and down

questions.explanation: Glossary defines crouch as bending knees and bringing the body down.

questions.question: Grandfather climbed the _______ with ease to reach the cage.
questions.explanation: He 'climbing the mailing [railing] with ease' to reach the tiger.

questions.question: The tiger Grandfather met at the zoo was described by the keeper as very _______.
questions.explanation: The keeper said: 'he is always very bad tempered.'

questions.question: Mahmoud fed Timothy mutton and _______.
questions.explanation: He was put on a diet of raw mutton and cod liver oil.

questions.question: What animal was Timothy's second companion?
questions.explanation: His two companions were Toto and a small mongrel puppy.

questions.question: Timothy was transferred to the zoo when he was about _______ months old.
questions.explanation: The text says 'when Timothy was about six months old a change came over him.'

questions.question: Grandfather discovered Timothy hiding among the roots of a _______ tree.
questions.explanation: He was found among the 'intricate roots of a banyan tree.'

questions.question: What did Grandfather 'shoe' away at the zoo?
questions.explanation: Grandfather 'shoed' the leopard away when it snarled at the tiger.

questions.question: The keeper stammered, 'But- sir- it is _______ tiger'.
questions.explanation: The keeper revealed to Grandfather that 'it is not your tiger.'

questions.question: In the summary activity, Timothy lived happily like one of the members of the _______ family.
questions.explanation: The summary activity mentions Timothy lived like a member of the writer’s family.

questions.question: Which of these words describes a 'slow relaxed / leisurely walk'?
questions.explanation: Glossary: stroll - a slow relaxed / leisurely walk.

questions.question: Grandfather admitted the _______ was very good in the jungle camp.
questions.explanation: He admitted the 'diner' (dinner) was very good despite being in a tent.

questions.question: How did Grandfather keep Timothy quiet in his old way?
questions.explanation: He 'smacked him across the mouth, which was his old way of keeping him quiet.'

questions.question: What color was the fur of the 'retriever' dog mentioned in the glossary?
questions.explanation: Glossary: retriever - a large dog with thick black or light brown fur.

questions.question: Who was the 'scornful look' directed at in the end?
questions.explanation: Grandfather gave the keeper a scornful look before walking out.

questions.question: Timothy appeared to be quite _______ of the puppy at first.
questions.explanation: At first Timothy appeared to be quite afraid of the puppy.

questions.question: Which animal would snarled at Timothy through the bars at the zoo?
questions.explanation: A leopard in the next cage snarled at him every now and then.

questions.question: Grandfather recognized the keeper who had been there when Timothy ________.
questions.explanation: He recognized the keeper from when Timothy first arrived at the zoo.

questions.question: Grandfather testily said, 'I realize he is _______'.
questions.explanation: He admitted to the keeper, 'I realize he is no longer mine.'

questions.question: What word in the glossary means 'run suddenly'?
questions.explanation: Glossary: dart - run suddenly.

questions.question: Timothy's favorite amusement was to stalk anyone who should _______ with him.
questions.explanation: One of his favorite amusements was to stalk anyone who should play with him.
